records.ety: [F. continence, L. continentia. See Continent, and cf. Countenance.]

quotes: He knew what to say; he knew also, when to leave off, -- a continence which is practiced by few writers.

defs: The restraint which a person imposes upon his desires and passions; the act or power of refraining from indulgence of the sexual appetite, esp. from unlawful indulgence; sometimes, moderation in sexual indulgence.
quotes: If they [the unmarried and widows] have not continency, let them marry.
Chastity is either abstinence or continence: abstinence is that of virgins or widows; continence, that of married persons.
